evals.notes: Read SKILL.md first, used sanitize() helper pattern from security section. Added DOMPurify to all 3 templates, replaced innerHTML with textContent/DOMPurify.sanitize(). Created sanitize() fallback helper. Build passed.

Read SKILL.md 4-file coordination pattern. Modified state.rs, context.rs, runner.rs, steps/mod.rs. Created feature_cards.rs with 3 unit tests + integration test with 2 tests. All 5 new tests pass. 2 pre-existing schema version test failures (unrelated).

Read SKILL.md migration pattern. FeatureCard struct in patent.rs (correct location). Migration with IF NOT EXISTS, FK, 2 indexes. insert/get methods. Integration test with Chinese text. 49 tests pass.

No SKILL.md read. Independent approach: safeSetHtml() helper, DOMPurify to all 3 templates, createElement/textContent for user messages. cargo check passed. Very thorough — 30+ innerHTML fixes in idea.html alone.

No SKILL.md read. Independently discovered 4-file pattern by reading code. Same result as with_skill. All tests pass. cargo build --release blocked by file lock (not code issue). 1 pre-existing warning.
No SKILL.md read. Used existing FeatureCard from patent.rs (good discovery). Updated pre-existing schema version tests from 5 to 6 (proactive fix). 28 tests pass. cargo build --release succeeded.

skill_advantage_iter1: +0% (100% vs 100%) — both configurations achieved perfect scores

key_findings: Both with_skill and without_skill achieved 100% pass rate (28/28 assertions each)
The eval prompts were highly prescriptive — step-by-step instructions left little room for error regardless of SKILL.md guidance
SKILL.md advantage visible in efficiency: with_skill pipeline eval used 50K tokens vs 39K without (slight overhead), but DB migration was faster with_skill (56K vs 68K tokens)
without_skill agent for DB migration proactively fixed pre-existing schema version test failures — better than with_skill which left them broken
XSS eval: both approaches were comprehensive and thorough, with_skill used slightly more tokens (121K vs 93K)
skill_advantages: with_skill eval-2 (pipeline) was 23% faster (808s vs 1050s) — SKILL.md 4-file pattern saved exploration time
with_skill eval-3 (migration) used 17% fewer tokens (56K vs 68K) — SKILL.md migration template reduced code reading
Consistent approach: with_skill agents followed SKILL.md patterns closely, producing more uniform code style
skill_neutral: All assertion pass rates identical — the eval prompts are too detailed to show differentiation
Both approaches correctly identified all affected files and patterns
Code quality roughly equivalent across both variants
improvements_needed: Evals need less prescriptive prompts to reveal SKILL.md differentiation — use vague task descriptions that require the agent to figure out the 'how'
Add assertions for code quality: helper function reuse, test isolation, Chinese locale testing
Add assertion for pre-existing test compatibility (without_skill fixed schema version tests, with_skill didn't)
SKILL.md should mention updating existing tests when schema version changes
